moves.note: Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety
Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% following scanner flag and to manage income allocation.
Increase QQQ to 25% for broader market exposure and stability.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into a more defensive sector.
Increase cash to 15% for capital preservation and buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for steady capital preservation, targeting around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces overall risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and increasing cash, but will limit upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stops and reduce leverage.
Trim QQQI to 10% based on the scanner flag and manage income exposure.
Add to CRWD, increasing exposure to a strong-performing AI/Software name.
Buy GOOGL at 10% to add a high-quality tech stock to the portfolio.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan prioritizes risk-first growth, aiming for around 2.5% per month. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality growth names, offering a moderate risk/reward profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stops and reallocate capital.
Trim QQQI to 5% based on the scanner flag, freeing capital for growth.
Add to SMH, increasing concentration in semiconductors given its strong scanner read.
Buy CRDO at 15% as a top-scoring accumulate setup for aggressive growth.
Buy ANET at 10% for its high risk/reward ratio and strong scanner read.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth plan targets around 4% per month by concentrating in high-scoring setups and leveraging strong sectors. Be aware that this approach significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs (if used) are subject to daily decay and rebalancing, which can lead to rapid losses if stops are not strictly adhered to.

moves.note: Exit high-beta single name for income

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and align with income focus.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce high-beta sector and single-name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 30% to boost income generation from covered calls.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and stability.
income.read: This income plan aims for steady monthly distributions, targeting around 1% per month, by rotating into income-focused ETFs. While prioritizing low drawdown, distributions are variable and not guaranteed, and the NAV can still decline in a market selloff. This is not a savings account.
